title: Anil Kumar vs Employees Provident Fund Organisation on 29 January, 2026
body: Anil Kumar vs Employees Provident Fund Organisation on 29 January, 2026 के ीय सूचना आयोग Central Information Commission बाबा गंगनाथ माग, मुिनरका Baba Gangnath Marg, Munirka नई िद ी, New Delhi - 110067 ि तीय अपील सं ा /Second Appeal No. CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 ANIL KUMAR ....अपीलकता/Appellant VERSUS बनाम CPIO, Employees Provident Fund Organisation Haryana ... ितवादीगण /Respondents Date of Hearing : 28/01/2026 Date of Decision : 28/01/2026 INFORMATION COMMISSIONER : Ashutosh Chaturvedi Relevant facts emerging from Second Appeal/Complaint: RTI application filed on 01/05/2024 CPIO replied on 30/05/2024 First appeal filed on 25/06/2024 FAA's order dated 23/07/2024 Second Appeal dated 30/09/2024 Information sought : The appellant has filed RTI application dated 01/05/2024 seeking the following information:- "1. That an enquiry u/s 7A of the EPF & MP Act 1952 initiated against M/s M.M. Wood Products, Village Mehmudpur, P.O. Bherthal, Bilaspur Road, Jagadhri, Yamuna Nagar bearing P.F Code No. HR/KNL/ 1841160 on 04.10.2019 by the then Regional P.F. Commissioner-II vide 7A Notice bearing No. Second Appeal/ Complaint No. - CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 Page 1 of 5 HR/1841160/Compliance/2025-2026 dated 04.10.2019 as establishment failed to make compliance of reminder letter No. HR/KNL/Comp-II/1841160/1396 dated 13.08.2019 and Show Cause Notice No. Comp./HR-KNL/DO/1841160/1554 dated 05.09.2019. 2.That since the date of initiation of 7A inquiry, proceedings are conducted by the Assessing Authority u/s 7A of the Act 64 times (approx) upto now, out of which the employer of establishment or any authorised representative on behalf of establishment did not attend the proceedings 38 times (approx.). To ensure the attendance of employer of establishment in 7A inquiry, Bailable warrant of employer issued, summon u/s 30 CPC 1908 were issued many times, Bank account was freezed, even fine were also imposed two time by the Assessing Authority u/s 7A of the Act. 3.That during these proceedings since the date of initiation of 7A inquiry, Assessing Authority has become changed 5 (five) times and similarly Enforcement Officer has become changed or squad of Enforcement Officers has become formed 5(five) times. 4.That during any proceeding since the date of initiation of 7A inquiry i.e. 04.10.2019, establishment M.M. Wood Products has failed to produce the attendance registers of its employees even of a single month and other required complete records during any proceeding in more than 04year and 6month before any Assessing Authority u/s 7A of the Act or before any Enforcement Officer/squad of Enforcement Officer upto now. 5.That since the date of initiation of 7A inquiry, every Assessing Authority u/s 7A and every Enforcement Officer/squad of Enforcement Officer are calling for the required records on each and every proceeding of 7A inquiry from establishment. 6.That since the date of initiation of 7A inquiry, more and more time were seeking for producing the required records by the establishment or representative of establishment on every proceeding which attended by establishment. 7.That moreover as per report dated 06.06.2019 of Enforcement Officers, establishment M/s M.M. Wood Products engaged 55 employees. As per online ECR filed by the establishment, establishment is depositing P.F. contribution only one or two persons since November 2020 upto December 2023. Since January 2024, establishment is not depositing P.F. contribution even of two employees also and is in default now. Whenever everyone is aware that a large number of workforces are required to run plywood factory. Second Appeal/ Complaint No. - CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 Page 2 of 5 8.That there is drastic failing down in the strength of employees from 43 to 02 employees of establishment M/s M.M. Wood Products after October 2020 suddenly. It means establishment M/s M.M. Wood Products is either on the verge of closer...." 2. The CPIO furnished a reply to the appellant on 30/05/2024 stating as under: "A public authority may refuse to disclose a record that contains information the disclosure of which could reasonably to be expected to be injurious to the enforcement of any law of India or a province or to the conduct of lawful investigation. Examples of the types of information to which this exemption may apply are illustrated as under:  Information relating to the existence or nature of a particular investigation.  Information that would reveal the identity of a confidential source of information.  Information that was obtained or prepared in the course of investigation". Therefore, keeping in view of above information sought cannot be disclosed. 3. Being dissatisfied, the appellant, filed a First Appeal dated 25/06/2024. The FAA vide its order dated 23/07/2024, held as under:- "उपरो थम अपील के बारे म CPIO- े ीय भिव िनिध आयु -II-अनुपालन शाखा, करनाल से चचा की गई तथा ात आ िक इस मामले म 7A िक कायवाही गित पर है तथा अंितम आदे श हेतु सुरि त है। तब तक धारा 8(1)(h) के अंतगत सूचना को deny िकया जाता है । CPIO ने भी इसी कार आवेदन का िन ादन िकया है। हालाँिक ाथ ने इसके िव केवल 8(J) का ही हवाला िदया है अपील िन ा रत की जाती है।" Being aggrieved and dissatisfied, the appellant approached the Commission by filing instant Second appeal on 30/09/2024. Further a written submission dated 02.01.2026 was provided by the Respondent stating, Second Appeal/ Complaint No. - CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 Page 3 of 5 "In this regard, it is to your kind notice that the information as sought by Sh. Anil Kumar, Appellant/Complainant vide his RTI application dated 01.05.2024 and vide First Appeal dated 25.06.2024 has been disposed off by CPIO, Regional Office, Karnal as the proceedings under section 7A is on progress in this matter and reserved for final order and till then information has been denied under section 8(1)(h) ." Etc.... 4. Relevant Facts emerged during Hearing: The following were present:- Appellant: Absent Respondent: Ram K Singh, EPFC- Assistant provident Fund Commissioner/CPIO participated in the hearing on VC. The Appellant has not availed the opportunity to appear before the Commission to plead his case. The Respondent submits that the RTI Application and the Appeal was disposed of by the CPIO as the proceedings under section 7A was not concluded and same was denied under section 8(1)(h) . Respondent further submits that another Application dated 11.10.2024 was received by Regional Office Karnal for the same information and the same was given to him vide this office letter HR/RO/KNL/RTI/Comp./18274 dated 28.11.2024. Further the file was personally inspected by the Appellant along with his Defense Assistant on 28.10.2025 and all the desired certified copies have been provided to Sh. Anil Kumar (AO/EO retd.). DECISION In the light of the facts of the case, the material on record and the submission made, Commission observes that that an appropriate response as per the provisions of the RTI Act, 2005 has been provided by the Respondent. Furthermore, written submission filed by the Respondent is comprehensive and self-explanatory. Thus information as defined under Section 2(f) of the RTI Act from available official records, has been duly provided to the Appellant, in terms of provisions of the RTI Act, 2005 . In the given circumstances, no further intervention of the Commission is warranted in this case under the RTI Act . The Appeal Stands Disposed of. Sd/- Ashutosh Chaturvedi (आशुतोष चतुवदी) Information Commissioner (सूचना आयु ) िदनांक/ Date: 28.01.2026 Second Appeal/ Complaint No. - CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 Page 4 of 5 Authenticated true copy (अिभ मािणत स ािपत ित) Ram Singh Meena (राम िसंह मीना) Dy. Registrar (उप पंजीयक) 011- 26715467 Address of the Parties: 1. CPIO ,RPFC-II/EPFO, Regional Office, Bhavishya Nidhi Bhawan, S.C.O 5-8, Sector-12, Karnal, Haryana-132001 2. ANIL KUMAR Second Appeal/ Complaint No. - CIC/EPFOG/A/2024/131991 Page 5 of 5 Recomendation(s) to PA under section 25(5) of the RTI Act, 2005:- Nil Powered by TCPDF (www.tcpdf.org)
